title: The Great Physician now is near (revised)
firstLine: The Great Physician now is near,
publicDomainBasis: William Hunter (1811–1877), John Hart Stockton (1813–1877). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
The Great Physician now is near,
The sympathizing Jesus;
Sin’s wounds, exhaustion disappear—
Draw quickly near to Jesus.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Your many sins are all forgiv’n,
If you will come to Jesus!
When conscience smites, you’ll peace be giv’n
When you draw near to Jesus!

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Corrupted nature, sin-stained heart,
All find their cure in Jesus;
All weakness, sighs, disease depart,
When you draw near to Jesus.

sections.label: Verse 3
Verse 4
sections.text:
Now healed and full released I am,
For I’ve received Christ Jesus;
All glory to the smitten Lamb!
I now believe in Jesus.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
My gracious Savior’s name’s so dear;
The name I love is Jesus!
Dispelling misery, so near,
No other name but Jesus!

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
When I’m that day to Him brought near,
Brought face to face with Jesus,
E’en more I’ll glorify and cheer:
O Jesus! Precious Jesus!
